channel.title: bloggy blog on alexis lavail
channel.description: Recent content in bloggy blog on alexis lavail

item.title: Catalan humour - Manuel Valls
item.description: Manuel Valls, a man you cannot trust, is coming back to France after a short stint in Barcelona politics, eyeing the next French presidential elections. Catalans seem happy to get rid of him. The video is a cover of this infamous song.

item.title: Bookmark clean-up
item.description: I haven’t gone thoroughly through my browser bookmarks in around a decade, and it was time to rummage and do a clean-up. The first realization is that around a third of all pages bookmarked do not exist anymore. This made me think about webpages longevity in general. The second realization is that there are tons of great random links in there that I just never went back to visit. Here are a few:

item.title: Typography
item.description: Typography is one of my nerdy hobbies. I do like a nicely printed book or magasine in a great typeface, and the same works for electronic content. There are so many high-quality free fonts around these days, that I changed the fonts used on this website many (many) times already. Now, in a low-tech effort, I decided to switch to default system fonts. This way, your browser does not download extra font-files when you browse here.

item.title: Standing desk
item.description: For a few years back, I have been doing some work from home, and bought a standard IKEA manual standing desk for the home office. This was a great buy, allowing to have good ergonomy while working at home. When the Covid pandemic started, we ended up being three people working from home, and I wanted a second desk where I could be standing. I decided to buy a Standsome slim, which I got to try by chance in the public library in Karlsruhe where I stopped to work a day during a train trip.

item.title: Les misérables
item.description: I recently re-read Les Misérables from Victor Hugo. As a Frenchperson, it is of course a must-read, having had to idolize Victor Hugo all through schoolyears and beyond. I am not the biggest fan, but I must say that the guy can write. Some sentences just resonate with me. À ses pieds ce qu’on peut cultiver et cueillir ; sur sa tête ce qu’on peut étudier et méditer; quelques fleurs sur la terre et toutes les étoiles dans le ciel.

item.title: Kungsängslilja
item.description: A pretty picture today, with one of my favourite flower: kungsängslilja in Swedish (and a great name in French: Fritillaire pintade). It is the official flower of Uppland province, which encompasses Uppsala.

item.title: Lappis humlegården
item.description: As a homebrewer, I am interested in the four basic ingredients that we combine to make beer: water malt hops yeast The tap water in Stockholm comes from lake Mälaren and is excellent. Malt I can buy locally from Swedish maltsters (e.g Warbro Kvarn). Amazing yeasts can be found too, like the Norwegian kveik yeast that can be dryed & re-used from batch to batch. What about hops though?

item.title: Covid
item.description: Well, I am sick this week, so nothing to be seen here. Come on, move along :-).

item.title: It's a start
item.description: Welcome to this blog! Hello world I guess. Why this blog? Well, thanks for asking. You provide some sort of peer-pressure so that I regularly write something. I suck at writing, and I dislike it. So this will be a good kick in the butt. Expect some science, much nonsense, some landscapes, beer-brewing and other misc. How often should I write? How often will I write? Who knows … But at least a post a week should be good, non?
